NKJV.1: Paul, an apostle (not from men nor through man, but through Jesus Christ and God the Father who raised Him from the dead),
NKJV.2: and all the brethren who are with me, To the churches of Galatia:
NKJV.3: Grace to you and peace from God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ,
NKJV.4: who gave Himself for our sins, that He might deliver us from this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father,
NKJV.5: to whom be glory forever and ever. Amen.
NKJV.6: I marvel that you are turning away so soon from Him who called you in the grace of Christ, to a different gospel,
NKJV.7: which is not another; but there are some who trouble you and want to pervert the gospel of Christ.
NKJV.8: But even if we, or an angel from heaven, preach any other gospel to you than what we have preached to you, let him be accursed.
NKJV.9: As we have said before, so now I say again, if anyone preaches any other gospel to you than what you have received, let him be accursed.
NKJV.10: For do I now persuade men, or God? Or do I seek to please men? For if I still pleased men, I would not be a bondservant of Christ.
NKJV.11: But I make known to you, brethren, that the gospel which was preached by me is not according to man.
NKJV.12: For I neither received it from man, nor was I taught it, but it came through the revelation of Jesus Christ.
NKJV.13: For you have heard of my former conduct in Judaism, how I persecuted the church of God beyond measure and tried to destroy it.
NKJV.14: And I advanced in Judaism beyond many of my contemporaries in my own nation, being more exceedingly zealous for the traditions of my fathers.
NKJV.15: But when it pleased God, who separated me from my mother’s womb and called me through His grace,
NKJV.16: to reveal His Son in me, that I might preach Him among the Gentiles, I did not immediately confer with flesh and blood,
NKJV.17: nor did I go up to Jerusalem to those who were apostles before me; but I went to Arabia, and returned again to Damascus.
NKJV.18: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to see Peter, and remained with him fifteen days.
NKJV.19: But I saw none of the other apostles except James, the Lord’s brother.
NKJV.20: (Now concerning the things which I write to you, indeed, before God, I do not lie.)
NKJV.21: Afterward I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
NKJV.22: And I was unknown by face to the churches of Judea which were in Christ.
NKJV.23: But they were hearing only, “He who formerly persecuted us now preaches the faith which he once tried to destroy.”
NKJV.24: And they glorified God in me.

ESV.1: Paul, an apostle — not from men nor through man, but through Jesus Christ and God the Father, who raised him from the dead —
ESV.2: and all the brothers  who are with me, To the churches of Galatia:
ESV.3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ,
ESV.4: who gave himself for our sins to deliver us from the present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father,
ESV.5: to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
ESV.6: I am astonished that you are so quickly deserting him who called you in the grace of Christ and are turning to a different gospel —
ESV.7: not that there is another one, but there are some who trouble you and want to distort the gospel of Christ.
ESV.8: But even if we or an angel from heaven should preach to you a gospel contrary to the one we preached to you, let him be accursed.
ESV.9: As we have said before, so now I say again: If anyone is preaching to you a gospel contrary to the one you received, let him be accursed.
ESV.10: For am I now seeking the approval of man, or of God? Or am I trying to please man? If I were still trying to please man, I would not be a servant  of Christ.
ESV.11: For I would have you know, brothers, that the gospel that was preached by me is not man's gospel.
ESV.12: For I did not receive it from any man, nor was I taught it, but I received it through a revelation of Jesus Christ.
ESV.13: For you have heard of my former life in Judaism, how I persecuted the church of God violently and tried to destroy it.
ESV.14: And I was advancing in Judaism beyond many of my own age among my people, so extremely zealous was I for the traditions of my fathers.
ESV.15: But when he who had set me apart before I was born,  and who called me by his grace,
ESV.16: was pleased to reveal his Son to  me, in order that I might preach him among the Gentiles, I did not immediately consult with anyone;
ESV.17: nor did I go up to Jerusalem to those who were apostles before me, but I went away into Arabia, and returned again to Damascus.
ESV.18: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas and remained with him fifteen days.
ESV.19: But I saw none of the other apostles except James the Lord's brother.
ESV.20: (In what I am writing to you, before God, I do not lie!)
ESV.21: Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
ESV.22: And I was still unknown in person to the churches of Judea that are in Christ.
ESV.23: They only were hearing it said, “He who used to persecute us is now preaching the faith he once tried to destroy.”
ESV.24: And they glorified God because of me.

NASB.1: Paul, an apostle (not [sent] from men nor through the agency of man, but through Jesus Christ and God the Father, who raised Him from the dead),
NASB.2: and all the brethren who are with me, To the churches of Galatia:
NASB.3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ,
NASB.4: who gave Himself for our sins so that He might rescue us from this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father,
NASB.5: to whom [be] the glory forevermore. Amen.
NASB.6: I am amazed that you are so quickly deserting Him who called you by the grace of Christ, for a different gospel;
NASB.7: which is [really] not another; only there are some who are disturbing you and want to distort the gospel of Christ.
NASB.8: But even if we, or an angel from heaven, should preach to you a gospel contrary to what we have preached to you, he is to be accursed!
NASB.9: As we have said before, so I say again now, if any man is preaching to you a gospel contrary to what you received, he is to be accursed!
NASB.10: For am I now seeking the favor of men, or of God? Or am I striving to please men? If I were still trying to please men, I would not be a bond-servant of Christ.
NASB.11: For I would have you know, brethren, that the gospel which was preached by me is not according to man.
NASB.12: For I neither received it from man, nor was I taught it, but [I received it] through a revelation of Jesus Christ.
NASB.13: For you have heard of my former manner of life in Judaism, how I used to persecute the church of God beyond measure and tried to destroy it;
NASB.14: and I was advancing in Judaism beyond many of my contemporaries among my countrymen, being more extremely zealous for my ancestral traditions.
NASB.15: But when God, who had set me apart [even] from my mother's womb and called me through His grace, was pleased
NASB.16: to reveal His Son in me so that I might preach Him among the Gentiles, I did not immediately consult with flesh and blood,
NASB.17: nor did I go up to Jerusalem to those who were apostles before me; but I went away to Arabia, and returned once more to Damascus.
NASB.18: Then three years later I went up to Jerusalem to become acquainted with Cephas, and stayed with him fifteen days.
NASB.19: But I did not see any other of the apostles except James, the Lord's brother.
NASB.20: (Now in what I am writing to you, I assure you before God that I am not lying.)
NASB.21: Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
NASB.22: I was [still] unknown by sight to the churches of Judea which were in Christ;
NASB.23: but only, they kept hearing, 'He who once persecuted us is now preaching the faith which he once tried to destroy.'
NASB.24: And they were glorifying God because of me.

NLT.1: This letter is from Paul, an apostle. I was not appointed by any group of people or any human authority, but by Jesus Christ himself and by God the Father, who raised Jesus from the dead.
NLT.2: All the brothers and sisters here join me in sending this letter to the churches of Galatia.
NLT.3: May God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
NLT.4: Jesus gave his life for our sins, just as God our Father planned, in order to rescue us from this evil world in which we live.
NLT.5: All glory to God forever and ever! Amen.
NLT.6: I am shocked that you are turning away so soon from God, who called you to himself through the loving mercy of Christ. You are following a different way that pretends to be the Good News
NLT.7: but is not the Good News at all. You are being fooled by those who deliberately twist the truth concerning Christ.
NLT.8: Let God’s curse fall on anyone, including us or even an angel from heaven, who preaches a different kind of Good News than the one we preached to you.
NLT.9: I say again what we have said before: If anyone preaches any other Good News than the one you welcomed, let that person be cursed.
NLT.10: Obviously, I’m not trying to win the approval of people, but of God. If pleasing people were my goal, I would not be Christ’s servant.
NLT.11: Dear brothers and sisters, I want you to understand that the gospel message I preach is not based on mere human reasoning.
NLT.12: I received my message from no human source, and no one taught me. Instead, I received it by direct revelation from Jesus Christ.
NLT.13: You know what I was like when I followed the Jewish religion — how I violently persecuted God’s church. I did my best to destroy it.
NLT.14: I was far ahead of my fellow Jews in my zeal for the traditions of my ancestors.
NLT.15: But even before I was born, God chose me and called me by his marvelous grace. Then it pleased him
NLT.16: to reveal his Son to me so that I would proclaim the Good News about Jesus to the Gentiles. When this happened, I did not rush out to consult with any human being.
NLT.17: Nor did I go up to Jerusalem to consult with those who were apostles before I was. Instead, I went away into Arabia, and later I returned to the city of Damascus.
NLT.18: Then three years later I went to Jerusalem to get to know Peter, and I stayed with him for fifteen days.
NLT.19: The only other apostle I met at that time was James, the Lord’s brother.
NLT.20: I declare before God that what I am writing to you is not a lie.
NLT.21: After that visit I went north into the provinces of Syria and Cilicia.
NLT.22: And still the churches in Christ that are in Judea didn’t know me personally.
NLT.23: All they knew was that people were saying, “The one who used to persecute us is now preaching the very faith he tried to destroy!”
NLT.24: And they praised God because of me.

WEB.1: Paul, an apostle (not from men, nor through man, but through Jesus Christ, and God the Father, who raised him from the dead),
WEB.2: and all the brothers  who are with me, to the assemblies of Galatia:
WEB.3: Grace to you and peace from God the Father, and our Lord Jesus Christ,
WEB.4: who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us out of this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father —
WEB.5: to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
WEB.6: I marvel that you are so quickly deserting him who called you in the grace of Christ to a different “good news”;
WEB.7: and there isn’t another “good news.” Only there are some who trouble you, and want to pervert the Good News of Christ.
WEB.8: But even though we, or an angel from heaven, should preach to you any “good news” other than that which we preached to you, let him be cursed.
WEB.9: As we have said before, so I now say again: if any man preaches to you any “good news” other than that which you received, let him be cursed.
WEB.10: For am I now seeking the favor of men, or of God? Or am I striving to please men? For if I were still pleasing men, I wouldn’t be a servant of Christ.
WEB.11: But I make known to you, brothers, concerning the Good News which was preached by me, that it is not according to man.
WEB.12: For neither did I receive it from man, nor was I taught it, but it came to me through revelation of Jesus Christ.
WEB.13: For you have heard of my way of living in time past in the Jews’ religion, how that beyond measure I persecuted the assembly of God, and ravaged it.
WEB.14: I advanced in the Jews’ religion beyond many of my own age among my countrymen, being more exceedingly zealous for the traditions of my fathers.
WEB.15: But when it was the good pleasure of God, who separated me from my mother’s womb, and called me through his grace,
WEB.16: to reveal his Son in me, that I might preach him among the Gentiles, I didn’t immediately confer with flesh and blood,
WEB.17: nor did I go up to Jerusalem to those who were apostles before me, but I went away into Arabia. Then I returned to Damascus.
WEB.18: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Peter, and stayed with him fifteen days.
WEB.19: But of the other apostles I saw no one, except James, the Lord’s brother.
WEB.20: Now about the things which I write to you, behold,  before God, I’m not lying.
WEB.21: Then I came to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
WEB.22: I was still unknown by face to the assemblies of Judea which were in Christ,
WEB.23: but they only heard: “He who once persecuted us now preaches the faith that he once tried to destroy.”
WEB.24: And they glorified God in me.

CSB17.1: Paul, <sup>ⓚ</sup> an apostle — not from men or by man, but by Jesus Christ and God the Father <sup>ⓛ</sup> who raised him from the dead <sup>ⓜ</sup>  —
CSB17.2: and all the brothers who are with me: To the churches of Galatia. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.3: Grace to you and peace from God the Father and our Lord <sup>[1]</sup> Jesus Christ,
CSB17.4: who gave himself for our sins <sup>ⓐ</sup> to rescue us from this present evil age, <sup>ⓑ</sup> according to the will of our God and Father. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.5: To him be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
CSB17.6: I am amazed that you are so quickly turning away from him who called <sup>ⓓ</sup> you by the grace of Christ and are turning to a different gospel —
CSB17.7: not that there is another gospel, <sup>ⓔ</sup> but there are some <sup>ⓕ</sup> who are troubling you and want to distort the gospel of Christ.
CSB17.8: But even if we or an angel <sup>ⓖ</sup> from heaven should preach to you a gospel contrary to what we have preached to you, <sup>ⓗ</sup> a curse be on him! <sup>[2]</sup>
CSB17.9: As we have said before, I now say again: If anyone is preaching to you a gospel contrary to what you received, <sup>ⓘ</sup> a curse be on him!
CSB17.10: For am I now trying to persuade people, <sup>[3]</sup> or God? <sup>ⓙ</sup> Or am I striving to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I would not be a servant of Christ.
CSB17.11: For I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that the gospel preached by me is not of human origin.
CSB17.12: For I did not receive it from a human source and I was not taught it, but it came by a revelation <sup>ⓚ</sup> of Jesus Christ. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.13: For you have heard about my former way of life <sup>ⓜ</sup> in Judaism: <sup>ⓝ</sup> I intensely persecuted <sup>ⓐ</sup> God’s church and tried to destroy it.
CSB17.14: I advanced in Judaism beyond many contemporaries among my people, because I was extremely zealous for the traditions of my ancestors. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.15: But when God, who from my mother’s womb set me apart and called <sup>ⓒ</sup> me by his grace, was pleased
CSB17.16: to reveal his Son <sup>ⓓ</sup> in me, so that I could preach him among the Gentiles, I did not immediately consult with anyone. <sup>[4]</sup>
CSB17.17: I did not go up to Jerusalem <sup>ⓔ</sup> to those who had become apostles <sup>ⓕ</sup> before me; instead I went to Arabia <sup>ⓖ</sup> and came back to Damascus. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.18: Then after three years I did go up to Jerusalem <sup>ⓘ</sup> to get to know Cephas, <sup>[5]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup> and I stayed with him fifteen days.
CSB17.19: But I didn’t see any of the other apostles except James, <sup>ⓚ</sup> the Lord’s brother.
CSB17.20: I declare in the sight of God: I am not lying in what I write to you.
CSB17.21: Afterward, I went to the regions of Syria <sup>ⓛ</sup> and Cilicia. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.22: I remained personally unknown to the Judean churches that are in Christ.
CSB17.23: They simply kept hearing: “He who formerly persecuted <sup>ⓝ</sup> us now preaches the faith <sup>ⓐ</sup> he once tried to destroy.”
CSB17.24: And they glorified God because of me. <sup>[6]</sup>

AMP.1: Paul, an apostle (not commissioned and sent from men nor through the agency of man, but through Jesus Christ—the Messiah—and God the Father, who raised Him from the dead),
AMP.2:
and all the brothers who are with me,
To the churches of Galatia:
AMP.3: Grace to you and peace [inner calm and spiritual well-being] from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ,
AMP.4: who gave Himself [as a sacrifice to atone] for our sins [to save and sanctify us] so that He might rescue us from this present evil age, in accordance with the will and purpose and plan of our God and Father—
AMP.5: to Him be [ascribed all] the glory through the ages of the ages. Amen.
AMP.6: I am astonished and extremely irritated that you are so quickly shifting your allegiance and deserting Him who called you by the grace of Christ, for a different [even contrary] gospel;
AMP.7: which is really not another [gospel]; but there are [obviously] some [people masquerading as teachers] who are disturbing and confusing you [with a misleading, counterfeit teaching] and want to distort the gospel of Christ [twisting it into something which it absolutely is not].
AMP.8: But even if we, or an angel from heaven, should preach to you a gospel contrary to that which we [originally] preached to you, let him be condemned to destruction!
AMP.9: As we have said before, so I now say again, if anyone is preaching to you a gospel different from that which you received [from us], let him be condemned to destruction!
AMP.10: Am I now trying to win the favor and approval of men, or of God? Or am I seeking to please someone? If I were still trying to be popular with men, I would not be a bond-servant of Christ.
AMP.11: For I want you to know, believers, that the gospel which was preached by me is not man's gospel [it is not a human invention, patterned after any human concept].
AMP.12: For indeed I did not receive it from man, nor was I taught it, but I received it through a [direct] revelation of Jesus Christ.
AMP.13: You have heard of my career and former manner of life in Judaism, how I used to hunt down and persecute the church of God extensively and [with fanatical zeal] tried [my best] to destroy it.
AMP.14: And [you have heard how] I surpassed many of my contemporaries among my countrymen in [my advanced study of the laws of] Judaism, as I was extremely loyal to the traditions of my ancestors.
AMP.15: But when God, who had chosen me and set me apart before I was born, and called me through His grace, was pleased
AMP.16: to reveal His Son in me so that I might preach Him among the Gentiles [as the good news—the way of salvation], I did not immediately consult with anyone [for guidance regarding God's call and His revelation to me].
AMP.17: Nor did I [even] go up to Jerusalem to those who were apostles before me; but I went to Arabia and stayed awhile, and afterward returned once more to Damascus.
AMP.18: Then three years later I did go up to Jerusalem to get acquainted with Cephas (Peter), and I stayed with him fifteen days.
AMP.19: But I did not see any other apostle except James, the [half] brother of the Lord.
AMP.20: (Now in what I am writing to you, I assure you as if I were standing before God that I am not lying.)
AMP.21: Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
AMP.22: And I was still unknown by sight to the churches which were in Christ in Judea (Jerusalem and the surrounding region);
AMP.23: they only kept hearing, "He who used to persecute us is now preaching the [good news of the] faith which he once was trying to destroy."
AMP.24: And they were glorifying God [as the Author and Source of what had taken place and all that had been accomplished] in me.

MSG.1: I, Paul, and my companions in faith here, send greetings to the Galatian churches. My authority for writing to you does not come from any popular vote of the people, nor does it come through the appointment of some human higher-up. It comes directly from Jesus the Messiah and God the Father, who raised him from the dead. I'm God-commissioned.
MSG.3: So I greet you with the great words, grace and peace!
MSG.4: We know the meaning of those words because Jesus Christ rescued us from this evil world we're in by offering himself as a sacrifice for our sins. God's plan is that we all experience that rescue.
MSG.5: Glory to God forever! Oh, yes!
MSG.6: I can't believe your fickleness—how easily you have turned traitor to him who called you by the grace of Christ by embracing a variant message!
MSG.7: It is not a minor variation, you know; it is completely other, an alien message, a no-message, a lie about God. Those who are provoking this agitation among you are turning the Message of Christ on its head.
MSG.8: Let me be blunt: If one of us—even if an angel from heaven!—were to preach something other than what we preached originally, let him be cursed.
MSG.9: I said it once; I'll say it again: If anyone, regardless of reputation or credentials, preaches something other than what you received originally, let him be cursed.
MSG.10: Do you think I speak this strongly in order to manipulate crowds? Or curry favor with God? Or get popular applause? If my goal was popularity, I wouldn't bother being Christ's slave.
MSG.11: Know this—I am most emphatic here, friends—this great Message I delivered to you is not mere human optimism.
MSG.12: I didn't receive it through the traditions, and I wasn't taught it in some school. I got it straight from God, received the Message directly from Jesus Christ.
MSG.13: I'm sure that you've heard the story of my earlier life when I lived in the Jewish way. In those days I went all out in persecuting God's church. I was systematically destroying it.
MSG.14: I was so enthusiastic about the traditions of my ancestors that I advanced head and shoulders above my peers in my career.
MSG.15: Even then God had designs on me. Why, when I was still in my mother's womb he chose and called me out of sheer generosity!
MSG.16: Now he has intervened and revealed his Son to me so that I might joyfully tell non-Jews about him. Immediately after my calling—without consulting anyone around me
MSG.17: and without going up to Jerusalem to confer with those who were apostles long before I was—I got away to Arabia. Later I returned to Damascus,
MSG.18: but it was three years before I went up to Jerusalem to compare stories with Peter. I was there only fifteen days—but what days they were!
MSG.19: Except for our Master's brother James, I saw no other apostles.
MSG.20: (I'm telling you the absolute truth in this.)
MSG.21: Then I began my ministry in the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
MSG.22: After all that time and activity I was still unknown by face among the Christian churches in Judea.
MSG.23: There was only this report: "That man who once persecuted us is now preaching the very message he used to try to destroy."
MSG.24: Their response was to recognize and worship God because of me!

NIV.1: Paul, an apostle—sent not from men nor by man, but by Jesus Christ and God the Father, who raised him from the dead—
NIV.2:
and all the brothers with me,
To the churches in Galatia:
NIV.3: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ,
NIV.4: who gave himself for our sins to rescue us from the present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father,
NIV.5: to whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
NIV.6:
No Other Gospel
I am astonished that you are so quickly deserting the one who called you by the grace of Christ and are turning to a different gospel—
NIV.7: which is really no gospel at all. Evidently some people are throwing you into confusion and are trying to pervert the gospel of Christ.
NIV.8: But even if we or an angel from heaven should preach a gospel other than the one we preached to you, let him be eternally condemned!
NIV.9: As we have already said, so now I say again: If anybody is preaching to you a gospel other than what you accepted, let him be eternally condemned!
NIV.10: Am I now trying to win the approval of men, or of God? Or am I trying to please men? If I were still trying to please men, I would not be a servant of Christ.
NIV.11:
Paul Called by God
I want you to know, brothers, that the gospel I preached is not something that man made up.
NIV.12: I did not receive it from any man, nor was I taught it; rather, I received it by revelation from Jesus Christ.
NIV.13: For you have heard of my previous way of life in Judaism, how intensely I persecuted the church of God and tried to destroy it.
NIV.14: I was advancing in Judaism beyond many Jews of my own age and was extremely zealous for the traditions of my fathers.
NIV.15: But when God, who set me apart from birth and called me by his grace, was pleased
NIV.16: to reveal his Son in me so that I might preach him among the Gentiles, I did not consult any man,
NIV.17: nor did I go up to Jerusalem to see those who were apostles before I was, but I went immediately into Arabia and later returned to Damascus.
NIV.18: Then after three years, I went up to Jerusalem to get acquainted with Peter and stayed with him fifteen days.
NIV.19: I saw none of the other apostles—only James, the Lord’s brother.
NIV.20: I assure you before God that what I am writing you is no lie.
NIV.21: Later I went to Syria and Cilicia.
NIV.22: I was personally unknown to the churches of Judea that are in Christ.
NIV.23: They only heard the report: “The man who formerly persecuted us is now preaching the faith he once tried to destroy.”
NIV.24: And they praised God because of me.

NRSVCE.1: Paul an apostle — sent neither by human commission nor from human authorities, but through Jesus Christ and God the Father, who raised him from the dead —
NRSVCE.2: and all the members of God’s family who are with me, To the churches of Galatia:
NRSVCE.3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ,
NRSVCE.4: who gave himself for our sins to set us free from the present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father,
NRSVCE.5: to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
NRSVCE.6: I am astonished that you are so quickly deserting the one who called you in the grace of Christ and are turning to a different gospel —
NRSVCE.7: not that there is another gospel, but there are some who are confusing you and want to pervert the gospel of Christ.
NRSVCE.8: But even if we or an angel from heaven should proclaim to you a gospel contrary to what we proclaimed to you, let that one be accursed!
NRSVCE.9: As we have said before, so now I repeat, if anyone proclaims to you a gospel contrary to what you received, let that one be accursed!
NRSVCE.10: Am I now seeking human approval, or God’s approval? Or am I trying to please people? If I were still pleasing people, I would not be a servant of Christ.
NRSVCE.11: For I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that the gospel that was proclaimed by me is not of human origin;
NRSVCE.12: for I did not receive it from a human source, nor was I taught it, but I received it through a revelation of Jesus Christ.
NRSVCE.13: You have heard, no doubt, of my earlier life in Judaism. I was violently persecuting the church of God and was trying to destroy it.
NRSVCE.14: I advanced in Judaism beyond many among my people of the same age, for I was far more zealous for the traditions of my ancestors.
NRSVCE.15: But when God, who had set me apart before I was born and called me through his grace, was pleased
NRSVCE.16: to reveal his Son to me, so that I might proclaim him among the Gentiles, I did not confer with any human being,
NRSVCE.17: nor did I go up to Jerusalem to those who were already apostles before me, but I went away at once into Arabia, and afterwards I returned to Damascus.
NRSVCE.18: Then after three years I did go up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas and stayed with him fifteen days;
NRSVCE.19: but I did not see any other apostle except James the Lord’s brother.
NRSVCE.20: In what I am writing to you, before God, I do not lie!
NRSVCE.21: Then I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia,
NRSVCE.22: and I was still unknown by sight to the churches of Judea that are in Christ;
NRSVCE.23: they only heard it said, “The one who formerly was persecuting us is now proclaiming the faith he once tried to destroy.”
NRSVCE.24: And they glorified God because of me.

NET.1: From Paul, an apostle (not from men, nor by human agency, but by Jesus Christ and God the Father who raised him from the dead)
NET.2: and all the brothers with me, to the churches of Galatia.
NET.3: Grace and peace to you from God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ,
NET.4: who gave himself for our sins to rescue us from this present evil age according to the will of our God and Father,
NET.5: to whom be glory forever and ever! Amen.
NET.6: I am astonished that you are so quickly deserting the one who called you by the grace of Christ and are following a different gospel —
NET.7: not that there really is another gospel, but there are some who are disturbing you and wanting to distort the gospel of Christ.
NET.8: But even if we (or an angel from heaven) should preach a gospel contrary to the one we preached to you, let him be condemned to hell!
NET.9: As we have said before, and now I say again, if any one is preaching to you a gospel contrary to what you received, let him be condemned to hell!
NET.10: Am I now trying to gain the approval of people, or of God? Or am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I would not be a slave of Christ!
NET.11: Now I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that the gospel I preached is not of human origin.
NET.12: For I did not receive it or learn it from any human source; instead I received it by a revelation of Jesus Christ.
NET.13: For you have heard of my former way of life in Judaism, how I was savagely persecuting the church of God and trying to destroy it.
NET.14: I was advancing in Judaism beyond many of my contemporaries in my nation, and was extremely zealous for the traditions of my ancestors.
NET.15: But when the one who set me apart from birth and called me by his grace was pleased
NET.16: to reveal his Son in me so that I could preach him among the Gentiles, I did not go to ask advice from any human being,
NET.17: nor did I go up to Jerusalem to see those who were apostles before me, but right away I departed to Arabia, and then returned to Damascus.
NET.18: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas and get information from him, and I stayed with him fifteen days.
NET.19: But I saw none of the other apostles except James the Lord’s brother.
NET.20: I assure you that, before God, I am not lying about what I am writing to you!
NET.21: Afterward I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
NET.22: But I was personally unknown to the churches of Judea that are in Christ.
NET.23: They were only hearing, “The one who once persecuted us is now proclaiming the good news of the faith he once tried to destroy.”
NET.24: So they glorified God because of me.
